# Battle Royale game PRD development (2026-03-06 19:36Z)

_**User**_

No podívej se. Pomáhám na tomhletom projektu kamarádovi. A on vlastně do těch her, co už tady v tom projektu existujou interaktivních, tak by chtěl přidat další hru. Jmenovalo by se to Battle Royal. A teď on má dobře namyšlený, jak by to mělo fungovat, ale moc s tím jakoby neumí pracovat. Rozepsal to do několika souborů a nedává to moc smysl. Já bych potřeboval, aby sis ty soubory v tý složce. To ti teď dám prošel. Zkusil z toho pochopit, jak to má celý fungovat. A napsat podrobný PRD podle toho, jak by ta hra měla fungovat a vypadat kompletně všechny ty její mechaniky. Aby jsme v tom neměli žádnou díru. Aby to fakt bylo hezky pokrytý, hezky vymyšlený, UX friendly. A až to napíšeš do toho PRD, já si to projdu. Maximálně udělám nějaké úpravy a potom by jsme to spolu vyrobili. Ale teď důležité je, aby sis prošel tu složku, všechny ty markdown soubory v tom, A napsal z toho jedno PRD, podle kterýho to pak postavíme tu hru.@battle-royale

Game type to send with the host join message. */\\\\\\\\n    gameType?: string;\\\\\\\\n    /** Player name sent with the join message (required for players). */\\\\\\\\n    playerName?: string;\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * sessionStorage key prefix for persisting player identity across redirects.\\\\\\\\n     * If provided, the hook will read/write &#96;lobbyPlayerId_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; and\\\\\\\\n     * &#96;lobbyPlayerName_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; from sessionStorage.\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    sessionKeyPrefix?: string;\\\\\\\\n    connectionStatus: 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected';\\\\\\\\n    socket: PartySocket | null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Shared hook that handles all PartySocket boilerplate for both\\\\\\\\n * host and player pages. Replaces ~60 lines duplicated in every page.\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function usePartyRoom&lt;TGame&gt;(\\\\\\\\n    roomCode: string | null,\\\\\\\\n    options: UsePartyRoomOptions = {}\\\\\\\\n): UsePartyRoomResult&lt;TGame&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const { asHost = false, gameType, playerName, sessionKeyPrefix } = options;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const [roomState, setRoomState] = useState&lt;RoomState | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n    const [gameState, setGameState] = useState&lt;TGame | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n    const [myPlayerId, setMyPlayerId] = useState('');\\\\\\\\n    const [connectionStatus, setConnectionStatus] = useState&lt;\\\\\\\\n        'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected'\\\\\\\\n    &gt;('connecting');\\\\\\\\n    const [socket, setSocket] = useState&lt;PartySocket | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if (!roomCode) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        const conn = new PartySocket({\\\\\\\\n            host: PARTYKIT_HOST,\\\\\\\\n            room: roomCode.toLowerCase(),\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('open', () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            setConnectionStatus('connected');\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            if (asHost) {\\\\\\\\n                // The host page must send a join with gameType.\\\\\\\\n                // When all connections drop during lobby→game redirect,\\\\\\\\n                // the PartyKit room resets to gameType='demo'. Without this,\\\\\\\\n                // start_game messages are silently dropped.\\\\\\\\n                conn.send(\\\\\\\\n                    JSON.stringify({\\\\\\\\n                        type: 'join',\\\\\\\\n                        playerName: 'Host',\\\\\\\\n                        ...(gameType ? { gameType } : {}),\\\\\\\\n                    })\\\\\\\\n                );\\\\\\\\n                setMyPlayerId(conn.id);\\\\\\\\n            } else {\\\\\\\\n                // Resolve identity: prefer saved sessionId from lobby redirect\\\\\\\\n                const roomKey = roomCode.toUpperCase();\\\\\\\\n                const sessionId = sessionKeyPrefix\\\\\\\\n                    ? (sessionStorage.getItem(&#96;${sessionKeyPrefix}PlayerId_${roomKey}&#96;) ??\\\\\\\\n                      undefined)\\\\\\\\n                    : undefined;\\\\\\\\n                const resolvedName = sessionKeyPrefix\\\\\\\\n                    ? sessionStorage.getItem(&#96;${sessionKeyPrefix}PlayerName_${roomKey}&#96;) ||\\\\\\\\n                      playerName ||\\\\\\\\n                      'Player'\\\\\\\\n                    : playerName || 'Player';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n                setMyPlayerId(sessionId || conn.id);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n                conn.send(\\\\\\\\n                    JSON.stringify({\\\\\\\\n                        type: 'join',\\\\\\\\n                        playerName: resolvedName,\\\\\\\\n                        ...(sessionId ? { sessionId } : {}),\\\\\\\\n                    })\\\\\\\\n                );\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('message', (evt) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            try {\\\\\\\\n                const data = JSON.parse(evt.data as string);\\\\\\\\n                if (data.type === 'sync') {\\\\\\\\n                    setRoomState(data.room);\\\\\\\\n                    setGameState(data.game);\\\\\\\\n                }\\\\\\\\n            } catch (e) {\\\\\\\\n                console.error('[usePartyRoom] Failed to parse message:', e);\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('close', () =&gt; setConnectionStatus('disconnected'));\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        setSocket(conn);\\\\\\\\n        (window as any).__partySocket = conn;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        return () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            conn.close();\\\\\\\\n            delete (window as any).__partySocket;\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n        // roomCode is the only stable dep; options are read once on connect\\\\\\\\n        // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\\\\\\\\n    }, [roomCode]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    return { roomState, gameState, myPlayerId, connectionStatus, socket };\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":126}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/jakubstastny/Cursor projects/personal/lesury/apps/web/hooks/usePartyRoom.ts\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"'use client';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\\\\\\\\nimport PartySocket from 'partysocket';\\\\\\\\nimport type { RoomState } from '@lesury/game-logic';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst PARTYKIT_HOST = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PARTYKIT_HOST || 'localhost:1999';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface UsePartyRoomOptions {\\\\\\\\n    /** If true, joins as a host (no playerName sent, no sessionId). */\\\\\\\\n    asHost?: boolean;\\\\\\\\n    /** Game type to send with the host join message. */\\\\\\\\n    gameType?: string;\\\\\\\\n    /** Player name sent with the join message (required for players). */\\\\\\\\n    playerName?: string;\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * sessionStorage key prefix for persisting player identity across redirects.\\\\\\\\n     * If provided, the hook will read/write &#96;lobbyPlayerId_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; and\\\\\\\\n     * &#96;lobbyPlayerName_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; from sessionStorage.\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    sessionKeyPrefix?: string;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface UsePartyRoomResult&lt;TGame&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    roomState: RoomState | null;\\\\\\\\n    gameState: TGame | null;\\\\\\\\n    myPlayerId: string;\\\\\\\\n    connectionStatus: 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected';\\\\\\\\n    socket: PartySocket | null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Shared hook that handles all PartySocket boilerplate for both\\\\\\\\n * host and player pages. Replaces ~60 lines duplicated in every page.\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function usePartyRoom&lt;TGame&gt;(\\\\\\\\n    roomCode: string | null,\\\\\\\\n    options: UsePartyRoomOptions = {}\\\\\\\\n): UsePartyRoomResult&lt;TGame&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const { asHost = false, gameType, playerName, sessionKeyPrefix } = options;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const [roomState, setRoomState] = useState&lt;RoomState | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n    const [gameState, setGameState] = useState&lt;TGame | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n    const [myPlayerId, setMyPlayerId] = useState('');\\\\\\\\n    const [connectionStatus, setConnectionStatus] = useState&lt;\\\\\\\\n        'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected'\\\\\\\\n    &gt;('connecting');\\\\\\\\n    const [socket, setSocket] = useState&lt;PartySocket | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if (!roomCode) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        const conn = new PartySocket({\\\\\\\\n            host: PARTYKIT_HOST,\\\\\\\\n            room: roomCode.toLowerCase(),\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('open', () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            setConnectionStatus('connected');\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            if (asHost) {\\\\\\\\n                // The host page must send a join with gameType.\\\\\\\\n                // When all connections drop during lobby→game redirect,\\\\\\\\n                // the PartyKit room resets to gameType='demo'. Without this,\\\\\\\\n                // start_game messages are silently dropped.\\\\\\\\n                conn.send(\\\\\\\\n                    JSON.stringify({\\\\\\\\n                        type: 'join',\\\\\\\\n                        playerName: 'Host',\\\\\\\\n                        ...(gameType ? { gameType } : {}),\\\\\\\\n                    })\\\\\\\\n                );\\\\\\\\n                setMyPlayerId(conn.id);\\\\\\\\n            } else {\\\\\\\\n                // Resolve identity: prefer saved sessionId from lobby redirect\\\\\\\\n                const roomKey = roomCode.toUpperCase();\\\\\\\\n                const sessionId = sessionKeyPrefix\\\\\\\\n                    ? : {}),\\\\\\\\n                    })\\\\\\\\n                );\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('message', (evt) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n }\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('close', () =&gt; setConnectionStatus('disconnected'));\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        setSocket(conn);\\\\\\\\n        (window as (rawGame as any).playQueue : [],\\\\\\\\n        scores: (rawGame as any)?.scores || {},\\\\\\\\n        status: (rawGame as any)?.status || 'setup',\\\\\\\\n    } as TheLineGameState;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const playerName = (id: string) =&gt;\\\\\\\\n        room.players.find((p: { id: string; name: string }) =&gt; p.id === id)?.name ?? id;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const playerAvatar = (id: string) =&gt; room.players.find((p: any) =&gt; p.id === id)?.avatar || '👤';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // Helper: get the live socket at call time to avoid stale closures\\\\\\\\n    const getSocket = useCallback(\\\\\\\\n        () =&gt; propSocket ?? (typeof window !== 'undefined' ? (window as any).__partySocket : null),\\\\\\\\n        [propSocket]\\\\\\\\n    );\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // ALL hooks must be declared before any conditional returns (Rules of Hooks)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // Auto-scroll to keep active slot visible\\\\\\\\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if ((game.status === 'playing' || game.status === 'revealing') &amp;&amp; timelineRef.current) {\\\\\\\\n            const container = timelineRef.current;\\\\\\\\n            const cardWidth = 208; // w-52\\\\\\\\n            const gap = 16; // gap-4\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            let targetIndex = game.cursorIndex;\\\\\\\\n            if (game.status === 'revealing' &amp;&amp; game.last_action) {\\\\\\\\n                const placedIdx = game.line.findIndex((e) =&gt; e.id === game.last_action?.eventId);\\\\\\\\n                if (placedIdx &gt;= 0) targetIndex = placedIdx;\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            const scrollPosition = targetIndex * (cardWidth + gap);\\\\\\\\n            const containerCenter = container.offsetWidth / 2;\\\\\\\\n            const scrollTo = scrollPosition - containerCenter + cardWidth / 2;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            container.scrollTo({\\\\\\\\n                left: Math.max(0, scrollTo),\\\\\\\\n                behavior: 'smooth',\\\\\\\\n            });\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }, [game.cursorIndex, game.status, game.activeEvent?.id, game.last_action?.eventId, game.line]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // Auto-advance timer when revealing\\\\\\\\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if (game.status === 'revealing') {\\\\\\\\n            setAutoAdvanceProgress(0);\\\\\\\\n            const startTime = Date.now();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            autoAdvanceIntervalRef.current = setInterval(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n                const elapsed = Date.now() - startTime;\\\\\\\\n                setAutoAdvanceProgress(Math.min(elapsed / AUTO_ADVANCE_DELAY_MS, 1));\\\\\\\\n            }, 50);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            autoAdvanceTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n                const s = getSocket();\\\\\\\\n                if (s) s.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'next_turn' }));\\\\\\\\n            }, AUTO_ADVANCE_DELAY_MS);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        return () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            if (autoAdvanceTimerRef.current) game.last_action?.eventId, getSocket]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // QR code generation for setup screen\\\\\\\\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if (game.status !== 'setup' || !canvasRef.current) host flows**\\\\n\\\\nI identified two host flow patterns: direct room creation for The Line/Demo, and lobby-based flow for Guessio via /host endpoint. I traced the host flow for Guessio: /host?game=guessio  RoomHost creates room  shows QR + player list  host clicks start  socket sends start event  server sets status to playing. I found that RoomHost redirects host to game page instead of rendering game UI because children() returns null. I'm tracing the player flow from join to game start and usePartyRoom with sessionKeyPrefix: 'lobby' reads the storage key incorrectly. I'm tracing the player flow from join to game start and discovered that Guessio requires sessionId in URL but Join redirect doesn't pass it. I found that Guessio requires sessionId in URL but Join redirect doesn't pass it, causing a redirect to /join when session is missing. Lesury platform\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&gt; **First:** Read [.agents/AGENTS.md](file:///home/clickout/Projekty/lesury/.agents/AGENTS.md) for project rules before proceeding.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n# Adding a New Game\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFollow these steps in order. Replace &#96;&lt;name&gt;&#96; with your game name in lowercase (e.g. &#96;quiz&#96;, &#96;trivia&#96;).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Step 1: Define types in &#96;game-logic&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCreate &#96;packages/game-logic/src/games/&lt;name&gt;/types.ts&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- &#96;&lt;Name&gt;GameState&#96; interface\\\\\\\\n- &#96;&lt;Name&gt;Message&#96; union type (all messages the game can receive)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Step 2: Write pure game logic\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCreate &#96;packages/game-logic/src/games/&lt;name&gt;/logic.ts&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- &#96;createInitial&lt;Name&gt;State()&#96; — returns starting state\\\\\\\\n- &#96;apply&lt;Name&gt;Message(state, message)&#96; — pure reducer, no side effects\\\\\\\\n- Any helper functions needed (e.g. &#96;getNextPlayerId&#96; is already in timeline/logic.ts)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Step 3: Create index and export from package\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCreate &#96;packages/game-logic/src/games/&lt;name&gt;/index.ts&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ts\\\\\\\\nexport * from './types';\\\\\\\\nexport * from './logic';\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAdd to &#96;packages/game-logic/src/index.ts&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ts\\\\\\\\nexport * from './games/&lt;name&gt;';\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Step 4: Write approval tests\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCreate &#96;packages/game-logic/src/games/&lt;name&gt;/__tests__/logic.test.ts&#96;.\\\\\\\\nCover at minimum:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Initial state shape\\\\\\\\n- Each message type\\\\\\\\n- Win/lose conditions\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nRun tests and promote snapshots:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\ncd /home/clickout/Projekty/lesury/packages/game-logic &amp;&amp; npm test\\\\\\\\n# Then promote:\\\\\\\\nfor f in src/games/&lt;name&gt;/__tests__/*.received.txt; do cp \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"$f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"${f/.received.txt/.approved.txt}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"; done\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Step 5: Register the game in the server\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIn &#96;apps/server/src/server.ts&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Import &#96;createInitial&lt;Name&gt;State&#96;, &#96;apply&lt;Name&gt;Message&#96;, &#96;&lt;Name&gt;GameState&#96;, &#96;&lt;Name&gt;Message&#96;\\\\\\\\n- Add the game type to the &#96;ServerState.game&#96; union type\\\\\\\\n- Add message type guard &#96;is&lt;Name&gt;Message()&#96;\\\\\\\\n- Add handler &#96;handle&lt;Name&gt;Message()&#96; — calls &#96;apply&lt;Name&gt;Message&#96; and broadcasts\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Step 6: Create frontend pages\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Host page** — &#96;apps/web/app/games/&lt;name&gt;/host/page.tsx&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\\\\\\\\nconst { roomState, gameState } = usePartyRoom&lt;NameGameState&gt;(roomCode, { asHost: true });\\\\\\\\nreturn &lt;NameHost state={{ room: roomState, game: gameState }} /&gt;;\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Player page** — &#96;apps/web/app/games/&lt;name&gt;/player/page.tsx&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\\\\\\\\nconst { roomState, gameState, myPlayerId } = usePartyRoom&lt;NameGameState&gt;(roomCode, {\\\\\\\\n    sessionKeyPrefix: 'lobby',\\\\\\\\n});\\\\\\\\nreturn &lt;NamePlayer state={{ room: roomState, game: gameState }} myPlayerId={myPlayerId} /&gt;;\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Step 7: Create display components\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- &#96;apps/web/components/games/&lt;name&gt;/&lt;Name&gt;Host.tsx&#96; — TV display\\\\\\\\n- &#96;apps/web/components/games/&lt;name&gt;/&lt;Name&gt;Player.tsx&#96; — phone controller\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSend messages via &#96;(window as any).__partySocket.send(JSON.stringify({ type: '...' }))&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Step 8: Build and test\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\ncd /home/clickout/Projekty/lesury\\\\\\\\nnpm run build   # must pass\\\\\\\\nnpm run test    # must pass\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":101}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/jakubstastny/Cursor projects/personal/lesury/.agents/workflows/add-new-game.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"---\\\\\\\\ndescription: Step-by-step guide for adding a new game to the Lesury platform\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&gt; ts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-06T20:14:19.260Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"thinking\\\":{\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"signature\\\":\\\"\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":30}\",\"8128948b-2ac6-4427-b383-a0da7827654d\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"b4f7dd3b-7a87-4d8f-993f-797bf89d4be4\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-06T20:14:19.260Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\\nHere’s a concise summary of the project structure and game architecture:\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n# Lesury Project Architecture Summary\\\\n\\\\n## 1. Project Architecture (Monorepo Structure)\\\\n\\\\n**Root:** npm workspaces (&#96;apps/*&#96;, &#96;packages/*&#96;), Turbo for orchestration.\\\\n\\\\n| Location | Purpose |\\\\n|----------|---------|\\\\n| **apps/web** | Next.js 16 frontend (React 19, Turbopack) |\\\\n| **apps/server** | PartyKit server (WebSocket, real-time sync) |\\\\n| **packages/game-logic** | Shared game logic (pure TypeScript, no framework) |\\\\n| **packages/config** | Shared ESLint/TypeScript config |\\\\n\\\\n**Build:** &#96;turbo run build&#96; — game-logic builds first, then web and server.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 2. Game Routing Pattern\\\\n\\\\nEach game follows a consistent route layout:\\\\n\\\\n| Route | Purpose |\\\\n|-------|---------|\\\\n| &#96;/games/&lt;game&gt;/&#96; | Landing page (how to play, host/join links) |\\\\n| &#96;/games/&lt;game&gt;/host&#96; | Host view (TV/screen) |\\\\n| &#96;/games/&lt;game&gt;/player&#96; | Player view (phone/controller) |\\\\n\\\\n**Games and routes:**\\\\n\\\\n| Game | Landing | Host | Player |\\\\n|------|---------|------|--------|\\\\n| **the-line** | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\\\\n| **guessio** | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\\\\n| **mindshot** | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\\\\n| **zoom** | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\\\\n| **timeline** | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\\\\n| **demo** | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\\\\n\\\\n**Host entry points:**\\\\n\\\\n- **Direct:** &#96;/games/the-line/host&#96;, &#96;/games/zoom/host&#96; — page creates room code if missing.\\\\n- **Via lobby:** &#96;/host?game=guessio&#96; (or timeline, etc.) — &#96;RoomHost&#96; creates room, shows QR, then redirects to &#96;/games/&lt;game&gt;/host?room=XXX&#96; on Start.\\\\n\\\\n**Player entry points:**\\\\n\\\\n- **Via lobby:** &#96;/join&#96; — enter room code + name, then redirect to &#96;/games/&lt;game&gt;/player?room=XXX&#96; when host starts.\\\\n- **Direct:** &#96;/games/the-line/player?room=ABC123&#96; — needs room code in URL.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 3. Game Component Pattern\\\\n\\\\nEach game has:\\\\n\\\\n- **&#96;&lt;Name&gt;Host.tsx&#96;** — shared display (TV/screen)\\\\n- **&#96;&lt;Name&gt;Player.tsx&#96;** — player controller (phone)\\\\n\\\\n**Props:**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\\\\n// Host\\\\ninterface TheLineHostProps {\\\\n  state: { room: RoomState; game: TheLineGameState };\\\\n  socket?: PartySocket;  // optional, can use window.__partySocket\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n// Player\\\\ninterface TheLinePlayerProps {\\\\n  state: { room: RoomState; game: TheLineGameState };\\\\n  myPlayerId?: string;\\\\n}\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**Sending messages:** &#96;socket.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'move_cursor', direction: 'left' }))&#96; or &#96;(window as any).__partySocket.send(...)&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n**Timeline subcomponents:** &#96;EventCard&#96;, &#96;ControlPad&#96;, &#96;ResultOverlay&#96; — used by &#96;TimelineHost&#96; and &#96;TimelinePlayer&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 4. Room / Lobby Flow\\\\n\\\\n**Two patterns:**\\\\n\\\\n### A. &#96;usePartyRoom&#96; (primary)\\\\n\\\\nUsed by the-line, timeline, demo, zoom, mindshot, guessio host:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\\\\nconst { roomState, gameState, myPlayerId, socket } = usePartyRoom&lt;GameState&gt;(roomCode, {\\\\n  asHost: true,\\\\n  gameType: 'the-line',        // for host\\\\n  sessionKeyPrefix: 'lobby',   // for player (persist identity across redirect)\\\\n});\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n- Connects to PartyKit room\\\\n- Sends &#96;join&#96; with &#96;gameType&#96; (host) or &#96;playerName&#96; (player)\\\\n- Listens for &#96;sync&#96; and updates &#96;roomState&#96; and &#96;gameState&#96;\\\\n- Returns &#96;socket&#96; for sending game messages\\\\n\\\\n### B. &#96;RoomHost&#96; / &#96;RoomPlayer&#96; (lobby wrappers)\\\\n\\\\nUsed by &#96;/host&#96; and &#96;/join&#96;:\\\\n\\\\n- **RoomHost** (&#96;/host?game=guessio&#96;): creates room, shows QR + room code + player list, “Start Game”. On start, redirects host to &#96;/games/guessio/host?room=XXX&#96;.\\\\n- **RoomPlayer** (&#96;/join&#96;): room code + name form, “Waiting for host…”. On start, stores &#96;lobbyPlayerId_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; and &#96;lobbyPlayerName_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; in &#96;sessionStorage&#96;, then redirects to &#96;/games/&lt;game&gt;/player?room=XXX&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n**Join flow:**\\\\n\\\\n1. Player goes to &#96;/join&#96;, enters code + name.\\\\n2. &#96;RoomPlayer&#96; connects via PartySocket.\\\\n3. Host starts → &#96;room.status = 'playing'&#96;.\\\\n4. &#96;RoomPlayer&#96; saves identity in &#96;sessionStorage&#96; and redirects to &#96;/games/&lt;game&gt;/player?room=XXX&#96;.\\\\n5. Player page loads, &#96;usePartyRoom&#96; with &#96;sessionKeyPrefix: 'lobby'&#96; reads identity from &#96;sessionStorage&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 5. Real-Time Communication\\\\n\\\\n**Stack:** PartyKit + PartySocket (&#96;partysocket&#96;).\\\\n\\\\n**Server:** &#96;apps/server/src/server.ts&#96; — PartyKit &#96;Server&#96; class:\\\\n\\\\n- Room = PartyKit room (room id = room code).\\\\n- First connection = host.\\\\n- &#96;join&#96; sets &#96;gameType&#96; and initializes game state.\\\\n- &#96;start&#96; sets &#96;room.status = 'playing'&#96;.\\\\n- Game messages routed by &#96;gameType&#96; to handlers (&#96;handleTheLineMessage&#96;, etc.).\\\\n- State broadcast via &#96;broadcastState()&#96; → &#96;{ type: 'sync', room, game }&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n**Client:** &#96;usePartyRoom&#96; in &#96;apps/web/hooks/usePartyRoom.ts&#96;:\\\\n\\\\n- Connects to &#96;PARTYKIT_HOST&#96; (e.g. &#96;localhost:1999&#96;).\\\\n- Sends &#96;join&#96; on open.\\\\n- Parses &#96;sync&#96; messages and updates state.\\\\n\\\\n**Message flow:** Client → &#96;socket.send(JSON.stringify(msg))&#96; → Server &#96;onMessage&#96; → game-logic &#96;apply*Message&#96; → &#96;broadcastState()&#96; → all clients receive &#96;sync&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 6. Shared Types and Game-Logic Package\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;@lesury/game-logic&#96;** exports:\\\\n\\\\n- **Room:** &#96;RoomState&#96;, &#96;Player&#96;, &#96;RoomMessage&#96;, &#96;generateRoomCode&#96;, &#96;validateRoomCode&#96;, &#96;generateRoomUrl&#96;\\\\n- **Games:** demo, the-line, guessio, mindshot, zoom, timeline\\\\n\\\\n**Per-game structure:**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\npackages/game-logic/src/games/&lt;name&gt;/\\\\n  types.ts      # GameState, Message union\\\\n  logic.ts      # createInitial*State(), apply*Message()\\\\n  index.ts      # re-exports\\\\n  __tests__/    # approval tests\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**Server:** Imports from &#96;@lesury/game-logic&#96;, calls &#96;apply*Message&#96;, never implements game rules.\\\\n\\\\n**Timeline:** Has types and logic in game-logic, but **no server handlers**. Server falls back to demo state for &#96;gameType: 'timeline'&#96; and does not handle timeline-specific messages.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 7. UI Framework and Styling\\\\n\\\\n**Stack:**\\\\n\\\\n- **Tailwind CSS v4** (&#96;@tailwindcss/postcss&#96;, &#96;tailwindcss&#96;)\\\\n- **shadcn/ui** (&#96;shadcn&#96;, &#96;radix-ui&#96;, &#96;class-variance-authority&#96;, &#96;tailwind-merge&#96;, &#96;clsx&#96;)\\\\n- **Framer Motion** for animations\\\\n\\\\n**Design tokens** (&#96;apps/web/app/globals.css&#96;):\\\\n\\\\n- Light: &#96;--background: #faf9f5&#96;, &#96;--foreground: #141413&#96;, &#96;--accent: #d97757&#96;\\\\n- Dark: &#96;--background: #141413&#96;, &#96;--foreground: #faf9f5&#96;\\\\n- Semantic tokens: &#96;bg-background&#96;, &#96;text-foreground&#96;, &#96;bg-accent&#96;, &#96;border-border&#96;, etc.\\\\n\\\\n**Components:** &#96;components/ui/&#96; — button, card, input, label, separator, skeleton, slider, badge.\\\\n\\\\n**GameLayout** (&#96;components/layout/GameLayout.tsx&#96;): Wrapper with optional back button, &#96;backUrl&#96;, &#96;backLabel&#96;, &#96;theme&#96; (light/dark).\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 8. Notable Details\\\\n\\\\n1. **Guessio player** expects &#96;?session=&#96; in the URL; &#96;/join&#96; redirect does not add it. Players coming from &#96;/join&#96; may be redirected back to &#96;/join&#96;.\\\\n2. **Timeline** has no server support; it would receive demo state and timeline messages would be ignored.\\\\n3. **Host flows:** The-line and zoom use direct host links; guessio and timeline use &#96;/host?game=...&#96; → RoomHost → redirect.\\\\n4. **Session persistence:** &#96;sessionKeyPrefix: 'lobby'&#96; lets players keep identity across the &#96;/join&#96; → game player redirect via &#96;sessionStorage&#96;.\\\"}\"},\"status\":\"completed\",\"context\":{\"composers\":[],\"selectedCommits\":[],\"selectedPullRequests\":[],\"selectedImages\":[],\"folderSelections\":[],\"fileSelections\":[],\"selections\":[],\"terminalSelections\":[],\"selectedDocs\":[],\"externalLinks\":[],\"cursorRules\":[],\"cursorCommands\":[],\"gitPRDiffSelections\":[],\"subagentSelections\":[],\"browserSelections\":[],\"mentions\":{\"composers\":{},\"selectedCommits\":{},\"selectedPullRequests\":{},\"gitDiff\":[],\"gitDiffFromBranchToMain\":[],\"selectedImages\":{},\"folderSelections\":{},\"fileSelections\":{},\"terminalFiles\":{},\"selections\":{},\"terminalSelections\":{},\"selectedDocs\":{},\"externalLinks\":{},\"diffHistory\":[],\"cursorRules\":{},\"cursorCommands\":{},\"uiElementSelections\":[],\"consoleLogs\":[],\"ideEditorsState\":[],\"gitPRDiffSelections\":{},\"subagentSelections\":{},\"browserSelections\":{}}},\"generatingBubbleIds\":[],\"isReadingLongFile\":false,\"codeBlockData\":{},\"originalFileStates\":{},\"newlyCreatedFiles\":[],\"newlyCreatedFolders\":[],\"createdAt\":1772825878438,\"hasChangedContext\":false,\"activeTabsShouldBeReactive\":true,\"capabilities\":[{\"type\":30,\"data\":{}},{\"type\":15,\"data\":{\"bubbleDataMap\":\"{}\"}},{\"type\":29,\"data\":{}}],\"name\":\"Game <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Excellent, now I have a thorough understanding of both the battle-royale concept from the markdown files and the existing project architecture. Let me now write a comprehensive PRD.

Let me synthesize what I've learned:

**From battle-royale files:**
- Battle Royale style board game on a 10x10 grid
- 2-4 players, each starts at random position with 3 HP
- Each round: Planning → Movement → Shooting → Zone → Damage
- Planning: secretly select 2 moves + 1 shoot direction
- Shrinking zone: 9 random border cells become orange (warning), then red (danger) next round


- Players lose HP from standing on red tiles or getting shot, and are eliminated at 0 HP—last player standing wins

**From existing architecture:**
- Monorepo with Next.js frontend, PartyKit backend, and shared game-logic package
- UI built with Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, and Framer Motion animations
- Real-time multiplayer via PartyKit WebSocket with a room system using usePartyRoom hook and RoomHost/RoomPlayer lobby components

Now I'm identifying the key gaps between the original prototype and this multiplayer architecture: the game needs to adapt from a single-device hot-seat setup to truly secret selections on individual player phones with the host viewing on a shared screen, and I need to nail down specifics like whether shooting is 4-directional, how far projectiles travel, whether movement means exactly 2 cardinal steps or allows staying still, and clarify the zone shrink mechanic—whether it's 9 specific border cells or 9 random cells each round.

I'm also working through the resolution order: do all players' moves happen simultaneously or sequentially, and if sequentially, in what order? Plus I need to define edge cases like what happens when players collide, move off-grid, move into red zones, and whether diagonal shots are allowed.

For the host/player split, the host displays the full 10x10 grid with all players, zone state, and HP on the TV while players use their phones to select their 2 moves from directional options.

The round flow would be: planning phase where all players submit their actions, then simultaneous resolution of move 1, move 2, and shots, followed by zone updates and damage checks before moving to the next round.

For shooting, I'm considering whether to stick with 4 cardinal directions or add diagonals for more strategic depth, and the projectile needs to travel in a straight line from the player's position.

For movement, each action lets a player step in a cardinal direction or stay put, with moves wasted if they try to leave the grid, and since damage happens in a separate phase, players can move into red cells but will take damage afterward.

The zone system cycles each round—orange cells warn of danger, then become red the following round. )
- Players on red cells at the end of the round take 1 HP damage
- "9 border cells" — starts from the border and shrinks inward, like a battle royale ring

Actually, looking more carefully, the original says "Zone shrink picks 9 border cells each round as warnings, which become red danger cells the next turn." So it specifically targets border cells. This makes the playable area shrink over time.

Let me think about this more carefully for good game design:
- Round 1: 9 border cells become orange
- Round 2: Those 9 become red (permanent danger). 9 more border cells (from remaining safe border) become orange.
- This continues, gradually shrinking the safe area

**Collision:**
- Two players can occupy the same cell
- No collision damage

**Timer:**
- Planning phase needs a timer to keep things moving — maybe 30 seconds
- Players who don't submit in time just stay put and don't shoot

For the player experience, I'm thinking the phone interface should display their current HP, a mini-map showing their position, directional buttons for both movement options and shooting, with a clear "lock in" button to confirm their actions before the timer runs out. On the host side, the big grid shows all player avatars in color with their HP bars, animated zone warnings in orange and danger zones in red, plus round counters and elimination announcements. I could also add spectator mode for knocked-out players and round summaries showing who took damage and why.

Now I'm considering some edge cases and design decisions: simultaneous deaths both eliminate players, stacked damage (zone + shot) applies both, and the shrinking zone naturally creates endgame tension. I'm leaning against a "skip shooting" option since forcing players to commit to a direction keeps engagement high. I should also think through how many danger cells expand per round to balance the pacing.

With 9 cells turning dangerous per round on a 100-cell grid, the board fills up in about 11 rounds, and since players have 3 HP they can survive roughly 3 rounds on danger cells—putting the absolute maximum game length around 14 rounds, which feels right for a quick game. The zone shrinks by eliminating the outermost ring of safe cells and moving inward, creating a nice circular compression mechanic that naturally funnels players together.

I'm debating whether to pick exactly 9 cells from the current border each round or use a simpler pre-defined shrinking pattern where the outermost ring becomes orange, then red, then the next ring inward starts turning orange. The pre-defined approach is cleaner to implement and easier to balance, so I'll go with that for the PRD.

Now I'm thinking through the action resolution order: moves and shots should both happen simultaneously so players can't react to each other in real-time — it's all about planning ahead. When two players shoot at each other, both projectiles pass through without colliding. The sequence is: all players move simultaneously, then all shots fire from their final positions, then we check for hits. I'm settling on simultaneous decision-making with sequential animation for fairness—all players plan their moves secretly, everything resolves at once logically, but the visuals play out one by one for clarity. Now I'm mapping out the full PRD structure, starting with overview and game setup, then moving through phases, mechanics, UI for both screens, win conditions, edge cases, and how it integrates with the existing system.</details></think>

PRD je hotové. Tady je shrnutí toho, co jsem udělal:

**Prošel jsem všechny 3 soubory** v `battle-royale/` složce — `readme.md`, `overall.md` a `rules.md`. Tvůj kamarád měl dobře vymyšlený základ, ale informace byly roztroušené a chybělo dost detailů. Hlavní díry, které jsem musel doplnit:

**Co chybělo a co jsem dodesignoval:**

1. **Host/Player split** — originál byl single-page HTML prototyp pro hot-seat hraní. Přepracoval jsem to na architekturu TV (host) + telefon (player), která odpovídá ostatním hrám v projektu.

2. **Planning Phase UX** — detailní popis, co hráč vidí na telefonu (mini-mapa, cross-layout ovladač pro směry, Lock In tlačítko), timer s defaulty pro AFK hráče.

3. **Simultánní resoluce** — vyjasnění, že všechny akce se řeší současně (ne sekvenčně po hráčích), ale animace se přehrávají postupně pro vizuální přehlednost.

4. **Střelba** — kompletní pravidla: projektil zasáhne prvního hráče v dráze, vzájemné zásahy fungují, Skip možnost.

5. **Zone shrink algoritmus** — konkrétní mechanika: 9 buněk z vnějšího prstence safe buněk, postupné zužování k centru.

6. **Edge cases** — odpojení hráče, simultánní eliminace, částečně vyplněné akce, multiple hits na jednoho hráče atd.

7. **Game state shape** — kompletní TypeScript typy pro integraci do `@lesury/game-logic`.

8. **Message protokol** — jaké zprávy se posílají mezi klientem a serverem.

9. **File structure** — přesný plán souborů a kde co bude v monorepu.

Projdi si `battle-royale/PRD.md` a dej vědět, jestli chceš něco upravit — pak to společně postavíme.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking about UX design for the player controls on the phone. The challenge is: the player needs to select 2 moves and 1 shoot direction. Having 3 separate D-pad/joystick controls would be cluttered and confusing. They want a cleaner, more intuitive solution.

Let me think about this carefully...

The core problem: 3 actions (Move 1, Move 2, Shoot) each with 5 options (Up/Down/Left/Right/Stay or Skip). Three D-pads side by side is ugly and confusing.

Ideas:

**Idea 1: Sequential single D-pad**
One D-pad that you use three times in sequence. You tap a direction, it gets "recorded" as Move 1, then the D-pad resets and you pick Move 2, then it resets again for Shoot. 

I like this approach — it's essentially a queue where your selections build up visually above the control. You'd see your action sequence displayed as a timeline showing each choice, and you could tap back on any of them to edit. Clean, minimal, and the visual feedback makes it clear what you're building.

**Idea 2: Swipe-based**
One D-pad area where you draw two swipes for movement and then tap a direction for shooting. Too gesture-heavy, error-prone.

**Idea 3: Two D-pads — one for movement path, one for shoot**
The movement D-pad records a "path" — you tap two directions in sequence on it. Like: tap Right, then tap Up, and it shows "→ ↑" as your path. Then a separate D-pad for shoot. This reduces from 3 D-pads to 2.

**Idea 4: Single D-pad with mode toggle**
One D-pad with tabs above: "Move" | "Shoot". In Move mode, you tap two directions sequentially. In Shoot mode, you tap one direction. Tabs show completion status.

I'm leaning toward the sequential single D-pad approach — it feels the most intuitive, like you're programming a robot by queuing up your three actions in order.

The layout would show an action queue at the top with three slots displaying your planned moves, and a single D-pad below that fills each slot as you tap directions, automatically advancing through the queue. You can tap any slot to go back and edit it, and the center button handles "Stay" or "Skip" options. It's clean and mobile-friendly with just one control on screen, and you get a full overview of your turn's plan at a glance.

I'm considering other approaches like drawing paths on a mini-grid or swiping gestures through directions, but those either violate the no-mini-map constraint or feel too finicky on mobile. The sequential D-pad with the action queue is the strongest option—it's intuitive, visual, and works perfectly for touch controls.

Wait, there's another possibility worth exploring: a command strip interface with three labeled cards at the top showing the move and shoot slots, with a single D-pad below that fills in directions sequentially and auto-advances through the cards. The center button would toggle between "STAY" and "SKIP" depending on context, and you could tap any card to go back and edit it.</details></think>

---

Tohle je super otázka a přesně ten typ věci, co rozhoduje, jestli bude hra intuitivní nebo frustrující. Máš pravdu — tři D-pady vedle sebe jsou matoucí a na telefonu by se na ně špatně mačkalo.

Nejlepší řešení podle mě je: **jeden D-pad, tři kroky postupně** — jako bys "programoval" svůj tah.

## Koncept: "Command Queue" + jeden D-pad

Představ si to takhle:

**Horní část obrazovky:** tři sloty/kartičky v řadě:

```
[🏃 Move 1]  [🏃 Move 2]  [🎯 Shoot]
     →             ?            ?
                   ▲
              teď vybíráš
```

**Spodní část obrazovky:** jeden velký D-pad (kříž se šipkami a tlačítko uprostřed).

### Jak to funguje:

1. Hráč otevře telefon, vidí 3 prázdné sloty nahoře a D-pad dole. Slot "Move 1" je aktivní (zvýrazněný, třeba s rámečkem nebo animací).

2. Hráč tapne směr na D-padu — třeba **Vpravo**. Slot "Move 1" se vyplní šipkou `→` a automaticky se přepne na slot "Move 2".

3. Hráč tapne **Nahoru**. Slot "Move 2" se vyplní `↑`, přepne se na "Shoot".

4. Hráč tapne **Vlevo**. Slot "Shoot" se vyplní `←`. Všechny tři sloty jsou plné, aktivuje se tlačítko **LOCK IN**.

### Klíčové detaily:

- **Tlačítko uprostřed D-padu** mění label podle kontextu: u Move slotů ukazuje **"STAY"**, u Shoot slotu ukazuje **"SKIP"**.

- **Zpětná editace:** hráč může tapnout na jakýkoliv vyplněný slot (kartičku) a vrátit se k němu — D-pad pak přepíše tu hodnotu a pokračuje dál.

- **Vizuální feedback:** vyplněné sloty mají ikonu šipky v barvě hráče, nevyplněné jsou šedé s otazníkem. Aktivní slot pulzuje nebo má výrazný rámeček.

- **Animace:** když vyplníš slot, kartička udělá subtilní "pop" efekt (scale up a zpět), šipka se fade-inuje. Přechod na další slot je plynulý.

### Proč je to dobré:

1. **Jeden ovládací prvek** — čistý, jednoduchý, na telefonu se na to krásně mačká.
2. **Progresivní** — hráč nemusí přemýšlet, co vybírá. Systém ho provede krok po kroku.
3. **Přehledné** — v horní liště vidíš celý svůj plán na jeden pohled: `→ ↑ ←`.
4. **Snadno editovatelné** — tapneš na slot, přepíšeš.
5. **Herní pocit** — připomíná "programování robota" nebo plánování tahů v taktických hrách. Je to satisfying.

### Celkový layout telefonu by pak byl:

```
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│  ❤️❤️❤️  Round 3      00:14  │  ← status bar + timer
│                              │
│  [→ Move1] [↑ Move2] [← Shoot] │  ← command queue
│                              │
│         [ ↑ ]                │
│     [←] [STAY] [→]          │  ← D-pad
│         [ ↓ ]                │
│                              │
│      [ 🔒 LOCK IN ]         │  ← confirm button
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

Tohle by se dalo rovnou zapracovat do PRD místo těch tří samostatných action selectorů v sekci 8.2. Chceš, abych to PRD aktualizoval, až přepneš do Agent režimu?

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking about adding configurable grid sizes for the host to choose from. They want options beyond just 10x10, including larger arenas. Let me think about what makes sense from a game balance perspective.

Key factors to consider:
- Number of players: 2-4
- HP: 3
- Zone shrink: 9 cells per round
- Movement: 2 moves per round
- Shot: unlimited range in cardinal direction

Let me think about different grid sizes:

**8×8 (64 cells)**
- Zone fills in ~7 rounds
- Small, intense, fast games
- Good for 2 players
- Players start close, action starts immediately
- Risk: might feel too cramped with 4 players


- **10×10 (100 cells)** is the current default and feels well-balanced across player counts
- **12×12 (144 cells)** gives more breathing room with ~16 rounds to fill, making it better for 4-player games where people want more tactical options, though shots become harder to connect
- **14×14 (196 cells)** stretches too long at ~22 rounds and creates too much empty space, which would probably kill the pacing

I'm leaning toward offering 8×8, 10×10, and 12×12 as the main options, skipping the 14×14 since it seems like it would drag on too much.

The key insight is that I should scale the zone shrink rate based on board size to keep game length consistent—so smaller boards shrink faster and larger boards shrink slower, all converging around 11 rounds to fill the zone.

I should also scale the minimum starting distance between players proportionally, so the board doesn't feel cramped on smaller sizes or too spread out on larger ones.</details></think>

---

Dobrý nápad. Tady je moje doporučení:

## Tři velikosti arény

| Velikost | Grid | Celkem buněk | Zone shrink/kolo | Plná zóna za ~kol | Charakter |
|----------|------|-------------|------------------|--------------------|-----------|
| **Malá** | 8×8 | 64 | 6 buněk | ~11 | Rychlá, intenzivní, hráči na sebe hned naráží |
| **Střední** | 10×10 | 100 | 9 buněk | ~11 | Vyvážená (současný default) |
| **Velká** | 12×12 | 144 | 13 buněk | ~11 | Strategická, víc prostoru na manévrování |

### Proč tyhle tři a ne jiné

**Klíčový princip:** délka hry by měla být konzistentní bez ohledu na velikost mapy — vždycky zhruba **11 kol** než se zóna zaplní. Proto škáluju počet shrink buněk proporcionálně (`boardSize² / 11`, zaokrouhleno).

**Proč ne 14×14 nebo větší:**
- 196 buněk je příliš — hráči by se skoro nepotkali, střely by většinou minuly. Hra by se táhla i s rychlým shrinkováním. Při 4 hráčích na 196 buňkách je hustota 1 hráč na 49 buněk — to je "hledání jehly v kupce sena".

**Proč ne 6×6:**
- 36 buněk pro 4 hráče je příliš těsné. Se 2 pohyby za kolo projdeš třetinu mapy. Nebyl by prostor na strategii.

**8×8 je ideální minimum:**
- Stále dost prostoru pro 2-3 hráče, ale akce začne okamžitě. S 4 hráči je to chaos — ale zábavný chaos.

**12×12 je ideální maximum:**
- Dost prostoru i pro 4 hráče, hra je víc taktická (víc plánuješ, kam se schovávat). Ale díky 13 buňkám shrink/kolo se nezpomalí.

### Co by se mělo škálovat s velikostí mapy

Kromě samotného gridu a shrink countu by se měly škálovat ještě:

- **Minimální startovní vzdálenost** (Manhattan distance): 8×8 → 2, 10×10 → 3, 12×12 → 4
- Zbytek (HP, počet pohybů, střelba) zůstává stejný — to drží komplexitu pod kontrolou

### Jak by to vypadalo v lobby

Host by v nastavení viděl vedle timeru ještě výběr velikosti:

```
Timer:     [15s]  [20s]  [30s]
Arena:     [Small 8×8]  [Medium 10×10]  [Large 12×12]
```

Výchozí by bylo **Medium 10×10** — to co je teď.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Let me critically review the PRD and identify any gaps, inconsistencies, or improvements.

**Things that look good:**
- Game identity, core concepts, grid, players, projectile - all well defined
- Game setup - clear flow
- Round structure - comprehensive with all phases
- Detailed mechanics - damage, elimination, movement, shooting rules
- Host screen UX - planning, resolution, game over
- Player screen UX - Command Queue design is excellent
- Visual design guidelines
- Game state shape - TypeScript types look good
- Message protocol - clear
- Technical integration - file structure, integration points
- Edge cases - comprehensive
- Game balance notes


- Now I'm spotting some inconsistencies and missing details. Section 5.5 claims "two things happen in order" but actually describes three—Activation, New warnings, and Zone damage—so that needs correcting. The Overview mentions a "10×10 grid" but should reflect that the grid size is now configurable. The landing page file is listed in the structure but never actually specified—I need to define what it displays, whether it shows how-to instructions or links to host/join modes. There's also a thought that got cut off, so I should check what else might be missing.

Looking at the gaps more broadly, I'm realizing there's no mention of sound or haptic feedback, which would really matter for a mobile game—haptic responses on D-pad taps, audio cues for shots and eliminations, zone warnings. The "Play Again" flow in section 7.4 is vague about what happens technically—does it reset state, keep players connected, return to lobby? Player color assignment in section 3.2 lists four colors but doesn't explain the logic—random, first-come-first-served, or player choice? And in Round 1, since there are no warning cells yet, the Zone Phase would only create new warnings without activation or damage, which should probably be called out explicitly even if it's implied by the rules.

I'm also wondering whether the zone should start shrinking immediately in Round 1 or if there should be a grace period where warnings don't appear until Round 2—though immediate shrinking seems reasonable since Round 1 warnings just become danger in Round 2. On larger boards like 12×12 with four players, the shoot animation delay of 2.5 seconds might need to scale based on projectile travel distance. And there's a clock sync question I need to think through: are the planning timer ticks server-driven with the client just displaying the remaining time, or does the client run its own countdown from an initial value? This affects whether players see consistent timers across different network conditions.

But the real issue I'm spotting is the privacy of player actions during planning phase. The game state broadcasts `actions` and `lockedIn` for each player, which means if the full state syncs to all clients, everyone can see everyone else's secret actions before they're locked in—that's a security problem. I need to make sure the server only broadcasts `lockedIn: boolean` to other players during planning, keeping their actual actions hidden. There's also the question of who can trigger the "Play Again" button and what happens if players disagree about continuing. I'm seeing a list of seven issues I found, with action privacy being the critical one that needs to be addressed in the design—the rest are refinements that would be nice to have but aren't blockers.</details></think>

---

Dobrá otázka — prošel jsem celé PRD pozorně a vidím pár věcí:

## Jeden důležitý problém

**Soukromí akcí během Planning Phase.** Tohle je zásadní: v game state máme `actions: PlayerActions | null` přímo na každém `BattleRoyalePlayer`. Jenže server broadcastuje celý state všem klientům přes `sync`. To znamená, že by si každý hráč mohl přečíst akce ostatních hráčů ještě během plánování — to rozbíjí celou mechaniku tajného plánování.

Řešení: server musí během planning fáze **stripnout** `actions` ostatních hráčů z broadcastu. Každý hráč vidí jen své vlastní akce + `lockedIn` boolean ostatních. Plné akce se odhalí až při resolution. Tohle by mělo být explicitně v PRD.

## Menší mezery

1. **Overview (sekce 1)** stále říká "10×10 grid" — teď když je velikost konfigurovatelná, mělo by tam být obecnější znění.

2. **Sekce 5.5** říká "Two things happen in order" ale pak listuje **tři** body (Activation, New warnings, Zone damage).

3. **Landing page** (`/games/battle-royale/page.tsx`) — je v file structure, ale nikde není popsané, co na ní bude. Ostatní hry mají info stránku s popisem hry a tlačítky Host/Join.

4. **Přiřazení barev** — sekce 3.2 listuje 4 barvy, ale neříká, jak se přiřazují. Automaticky podle pořadí připojení? Náhodně? Výběr hráče?

5. **"Play Again" flow** (sekce 7.4) — kdo ho může spustit? Jen host? Co se technicky stane — reset state, hráči zůstanou připojení?

6. **Zvuky / haptic feedback** — pro mobilní hru by bylo dobré alespoň zmínit: haptic feedback při tapnutí D-padu, zvukový efekt při zásahu, eliminaci, zone damage, timer warning. I kdyby to bylo "nice to have", mělo by to být v PRD.

---

They think I'm talking about a hacking scenario, but since these are just friends playing a board game, it's not a concern. They have a point - the data is technically in the sync payload, but the UI won't display it. For a casual board game among friends, this is probably fine. I should acknowledge this but maybe mention it briefly as a note.

The Vibration API is available in most mobile browsers like Chrome and Firefox on Android, plus Safari on iOS with some limitations. I can use `navigator.vibrate()` for simple haptic feedback that works well for short taps, though iOS Safari has limited support and doesn't fully support the Vibration API.

Actually, let me clarify - `navigator.vibrate()` works reliably on Android Chrome but isn't supported on iOS Safari at all. For iOS, I could use CSS `:active` state changes for visual feedback or explore experimental APIs, but the reality is haptic feedback would only work consistently on Android. I should present this as a progressive enhancement that gracefully degrades on unsupported devices.</details></think>

Jasně, rozumím. Pojďme to projít:

**Soukromí akcí** — máš pravdu, pro kamarády co si chtějí zahrát deskovku to není problém. Akce se prostě v UI nezobrazí ostatním a hotovo. Nebudu to řešit jako bezpečnostní concern.

**Haptic feedback v prohlížeči** — dobrá otázka. Krátká odpověď: **jde to, ale jen na Androidu**. Prohlížeče mají `navigator.vibrate()` API, které umožňuje krátké vibrace (třeba 50ms pulse při tapnutí D-padu). Na **Android Chrome** to funguje spolehlivě. Na **iOS Safari** to bohužel **nefunguje** — Apple toto API nepodporuje.

Dá se to ale udělat jako progressive enhancement — kde to jde, zavibruje, kde ne, nic se nestane. Implementačně je to jeden řádek:

```typescript
navigator.vibrate?.(50);
```

Takže bych to do PRD dal jako "haptic feedback where supported (Android)" — nula práce navíc, ale zlepší to pocit na Androidu.

3. **Landing page** — doplnit popis co tam bude (krátký popis hry, pravidla, tlačítka Host/Join)
4. **Barvy** — doplnit: náhodné přiřazení
5. **Play Again** — doplnit: jen host může spustit
6. **Haptic feedback** — přidat jako progressive enhancement (Android only)

Prošel jsem to celé ještě jednou a myslím, že je to PRD skutečně kompletní. Pokrývá:

- Herní mechaniky (pohyb, střelba, zóna, eliminace, damage stacking)
- Kompletní round flow se všemi fázemi
- Konfigurovatelnou arenu i timer
- UX pro host (TV) i hráče (telefon) včetně Command Queue designu
- Landing page
- Game state typy v TypeScriptu
- Message protokol (client ↔ server)
- File strukturu a integraci do existujícího monorepa
- Edge cases (14 scénářů)
- Balance notes

Jediná drobnost, kterou bych zmínil, ale **není nutná do PRD** — je to spíš implementační detail: až budete stavět, bude potřeba rozhodnout, jestli se planning timer počítá na serveru a broadcastuje klientům (přesnější, ale náročnější na síť), nebo jestli server pošle jen "planning started at timestamp" a klienti si timer počítají sami. Ale to je věc, kterou vyřešíte při implementaci, ne v PRD.

Takže jo — PRD je ready. Můžete podle něj začít stavět.
